items.correct: Subtle hints or clues earlier in a text that anticipate later events, making the resolution feel both surprising and inevitable.
items.misconception: Foreshadowing is when a character predicts the future.
items.random_wrong: Foreshadowing is the shadow a character casts in a scene.
items.why: Foreshadowing works on the reader, not within the story world. A character's prediction is a plot event — foreshadowing is an authorial technique embedded in detail and tone.

items.q: What is the structure of a Shakespearean sonnet?
items.correct: Fourteen lines in iambic pentameter, structured as three quatrains and a final couplet, rhyming ABAB CDCD EFEF GG, often with a volta near the couplet.
items.misconception: A sonnet is any short love poem.
items.random_wrong: A sonnet must always be exactly ten lines long.
items.why: Students treat sonnet as a vague genre. It is a strict 14-line form with specific rhyme schemes — and sonnets need not be about love (think Hopkins's religious sonnets).

items.correct: Self-aware metafiction, intertextuality, scepticism toward grand narratives, playfulness with form, and blurring of high/low culture.
items.misconception: Postmodernism just means literature written after modernism.
items.random_wrong: Postmodernism means writing about the future.
items.why: Postmodernism is a specific set of attitudes and techniques, not a date range. A novel written in 2024 may be entirely traditional and not postmodern in any meaningful sense.

items.q: Which Assessment Objective covers analysis of language, form and structure?
items.why: Correct answer: AO2. “AO1” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.correct: It identifies a tension or argument
items.why: The answer examiners want is: It identifies a tension or argument. “It is long” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Roughly how many minutes does give for the anthology comparison question?
items.why: The real figure is 45. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
